event: Altar of Archangel Michael defeating Satan
symbols: Michael piercing the dragon — the cosmic victory of heaven over hell, the defeat of Satan
Dragon/demon underfoot — evil subdued and trampled by divine authority
Cross held by the saint on the left panel — the instrument of salvation and victory over death
Franciscan habit on the right — the religious life dedicated to imitating Christ in poverty and holiness
description: A triptych altarpiece with three panels. The central panel dominates: Archangel Michael in flowing red and blue robes with large dark wings stands atop a monstrous dragon-like demon, thrusting a spear or cross-staff into it. Above him, smaller angels descend from heavenly clouds. The left panel shows a bearded figure in red robes holding a golden cross. The right panel depicts a tonsured friar in a gray Franciscan habit holding a book, with a landscape behind him. All three panels have rocky landscape backgrounds.
theological_description: The Archangel Michael's combat with Satan is rooted in Revelation 12:7-9: 'And there was war in heaven. Michael and his angels fought against the dragon... that ancient serpent called the devil.' Michael is the heavenly champion, the prince who protects God's people (DAN.12.1). His victory over the dragon is the celestial counterpart to Christ's victory over sin and death on the cross. Jude 1:9 records Michael contending with the devil over the body of Moses, further establishing his role as heaven's warrior. The flanking saints represent the earthly Church militant — those who follow Christ by taking up the cross and living in poverty and obedience. The entire composition proclaims the triumph of good over evil at both cosmic and personal levels, affirming Paul's promise: 'The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet' (ROM.16.20).

scripture_refs.reason: Michael and his angels fight against the dragon and cast him out of heaven — the central scene
Michael the great prince who protects God's people in the time of distress
Michael contending with the devil, establishing his role as heaven's champion against evil
The God of peace will crush Satan underfoot — the promise fulfilled in Michael's cosmic victory
The proto-evangelium of the seed crushing the serpent's head, enacted in Michael's defeat of the dragon
